title: Compare decimals and fractions in different forms
description: Compare fractions and decimals in different forms, such as grids or word form. Decimals and fractions in these problems are limited to tenths and hundredths.
topic_path: Compare decimals and fractions in different forms

assessment_items.question_content:
**Compare with $>,<,$ or $=$.**


$2.09$ [[☃ dropdown 1]] $1$ one and $19$ tenths
hints:
Let's compare $\pink{2.09}$ and $\blueD{1\text{ one and } 19 \text{ tenths}}$ with visuals.  
*(Each big square represents one whole.)*

$\pink{2.09}:$|![A big square is completely shaded pink.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cd34526d153208c0fb2c5884b5ca657d51e1c147) ![A big square is completely shaded pink.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cd34526d153208c0fb2c5884b5ca657d51e1c147) ![A big square divided into 100 equal sized squares that are set in 10 rows of 10 squares each. In the bottom row, the left 9 squares are shaded pink.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/acd180541221eb345f85188137c88cfbae894a27)
- | - | -
$\blueD{1\text{ one and } 19 \text{ tenths}}:$ | ![A big square is completely shaded blue.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2608fd9f2206158155e1878d7011dff5ce40b91a) ![A big square divided into 10 equal sized rectangles that are set in 10 rows of 1 rectangle each. All 10 rows with rectangles are shaded blue.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/56cdf4ffa6b3592914737fdd64959ccf85f99c6f) ![A big square divided into 10 equal sized rectangles that are set in 10 rows of 1 rectangle each. 9 bottom rows with rectangles are shaded blue.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3a6746820919f4d8f3490a0b69e768be177902be)
$\pink{2.09}$ covers **less** area than $\blue{2.9}$, so $\pink{2.09}$ is **less** than $\blue{2.9}$.
$\pink{2.09}< \blue{1 \text{ one and }19 \text{ tenths}}$

Let's rewrite $\blue{\dfrac6{10}}$ as a decimal number:

$\blue{\dfrac6{10}} = \blue{6 \text{ tenths}}= \blue{0.6}$

Now we can compare $\pink{0.54}$ and $\blue{0.6}$ with visuals.  
*(Each big square represents one whole.)*

$\pink{0.54}:$| ![A big square divided into 100 equal sized squares that are set in 10 rows of 10 squares each. In the bottom 5 rows, all 10 squares are shaded pink. In the sixth row from the bottom, the left 4 squares are shaded pink.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/bdb54acfb1a9c3039632b0f2886cf28969925f83)
- | - | -
$\blue{0.6}:$ | ![A big square divided into 10 equal sized rectangles that are set in 10 rows of 1 rectangle each. 6 bottom rows with rectangles are shaded blue.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/bbc388d7b97d061d3c0ce6f11dcfff61e6bb428b)
$\pink{0.54}$ covers **less** area than $\blue{0.6}$, so $\pink{0.54}$ is **less** than $\blue{0.6}$.
$\pink{0.54}< \blue{\dfrac6{10}}$

Let's rewrite $\blue{2 \text{ tenths}}$ as a decimal number:

$\blue{2\text{ tenths}}= \blue{0.2}$

Now we can compare $\pink{0.08}$ and $\blue{0.2}$ with visuals.  
*(Each big square represents one whole.)*

$\pink{0.08}:$| ![A big square divided into 100 equal sized squares that are set in 10 rows of 10 squares each. In the bottom row, the left 8 squares are shaded pink.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ecee3a31dc6fa01135e68210999d663ef73d2db5)
- | - | -
$\blue{0.2}:$ | ![A big square divided into 10 equal sized rectangles that are set in 10 rows of 1 rectangle each. 2 bottom rows with rectangles are shaded blue.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/48899072797be3e9bcbc22813e85da6967a5ad52)
$\pink{0.08}$ covers **less** area than $\blue{0.2}$, so $\pink{0.08}$ is **less** than $\blue{0.2}$.
$\pink{0.08}< \blue{2\text{ tenths}}$

First, let's write our visual as a decimal.

[[☃ image 1]]
 $=0.79$

Now we can compare $\blue{1.09}$ and $\pink{0.79}$ with visuals.  
*(Each big square represents one whole.)*

$\pink{0.79}:$| ![A big square divided into 100 equal sized squares that are set in 10 rows of 10 squares each. In the bottom 7 rows, all 10 squares are shaded pink. In the eighth row from the bottom, the left 9 squares are shaded pink.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6410d2e9115c25bf31be81ebfae0e80d9b9cf3f8)
- | - | -
$\blue{1.09}:$| ![A big square is completely shaded blue.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2608fd9f2206158155e1878d7011dff5ce40b91a) ![A big square divided into 100 equal sized squares that are set in 10 rows of 10 squares each. In the bottom row, the left 9 squares are shaded blue.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2e425c1dd0ccd636e7039cd0022681a19780841b)
$\pink{0.79}$ covers **less** area than $\blueD{1.09}$, so $\pink{0.79}$ is **less** than $\blueD{1.09}$.

options.alt: A square divided into 100 equal sized squares that are set in 10 rows of 10 squares each. In the bottom 7 rows, all 10 squares are shaded pink. In the eighth row from the bottom, the left 9 squares are shaded pink.

hints:
First, let's write our visual as a fraction.

[[☃ image 1]]
$=\dfrac{6}{10}$

Now we can compare $\blueD{\dfrac{6}{10}}$ and $\pink{\dfrac{8}{100}}$ with visuals.  
*(Each big square represents one whole.)*

$\pink{\dfrac{8}{100}}:$|![A big square divided into 100 equal sized squares that are set in 10 rows of 10 squares each. In the bottom row, the left 8 squares are shaded pink.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ee328f5a52a343f3292a10e124299e08d309401d)
- | - | -
$\blueD{\dfrac{6}{10}}:$| ![A big square divided into 10 equal sized rectangles that are set in 10 rows of 1 rectangle each. 6 bottom rows with rectangles are shaded blue.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b19e3a044b71c17898443d00109cbcd328b6ce63)
$\pink{\dfrac{8}{100}}$ covers **less** area than $\blueD{\dfrac{6}{10}}$, so $\pink{\dfrac{8}{100}}$ is **less** than $\blueD{\dfrac{6}{10}}$.

$\pink{\dfrac{8}{100}} <$ 

[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: A big square divided into 10 equal sized rectangles that are set in 10 rows of 1 rectangle each. 6 bottom rows with rectangles are shaded blue.

hints:
Let's compare $\pink{1.3}$ and $\blueD{13\text{ tenths }}$ with visuals.  
*(Each big square represents one whole.)*

$\pink{1.3}:$|![A big square is completely shaded pink.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cd34526d153208c0fb2c5884b5ca657d51e1c147) ![A big square divided into 10 equal sized rectangles that are set in 10 rows of 1 rectangle each. 3 bottom rows with rectangles are shaded pink.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f2514faa641659b4e7eb410dea0158b9e6665484)
- | - | -
$\blueD{13\text{ tenths}}:$ | ![A big square divided into 10 equal sized rectangles that are set in 10 rows of 1 rectangle each. All 10 rows with rectangles are shaded blue.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/56cdf4ffa6b3592914737fdd64959ccf85f99c6f) ![A big square divided into 10 equal sized rectangles that are set in 10 rows of 1 rectangle each. 3 bottom rows with rectangles are shaded blue.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c360ee792e31f138c6487189be94b1823ce5223b)

Let's rewrite $\blueD{13\text{ tenths}}$ as a decimal number:

$\blueD{13\text{ tenths}}= \blueD{1.3}$
$\pink{1.3}= \blueD{13\text{ tenths}}$

Let's rewrite $\blue{\dfrac{114}{100}}$ as a decimal number:

$\blue{\dfrac{114}{100}} = \blue{114 \text{ hundredths}}= \blue{1.14}$
Now we can compare $\blue{1.14}$ and $\pink{1.14}$ with visuals.  
*(Each big square represents one whole.)*

$\blue{1.14}:$| ![A big square is completely shaded blue.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2608fd9f2206158155e1878d7011dff5ce40b91a) ![A big square divided into 100 equal sized squares that are set in 10 rows of 10 squares each. In the bottom row, all 10 squares are shaded blue. In the second row from the bottom, the left 4 squares are shaded blue.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6e520de2f4edd423a6db1d5ac2a264a2befbba04)
- | - | -
$\pink{1.14}:$ | ![A big square is completely shaded pink.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cd34526d153208c0fb2c5884b5ca657d51e1c147)![A big square divided into 100 equal sized squares that are set in 10 rows of 10 squares each. In the bottom row, all 10 squares are shaded pink. In the second row from the bottom, the left 4 squares are shaded pink.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ef21b287848017553f3745b02267a7c44e2b24d4)
$\blue{1.14} = \pink{1.14}$
$\blue{\dfrac{114}{100}} = \pink{1.14}$

Let's compare $\pink{2.7}$ and $\blue{\dfrac{217}{100}}$ with visuals.  
*(Each big square represents one whole.)*

$\pink{2.7}:$| ![A big square is completely shaded pink.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cd34526d153208c0fb2c5884b5ca657d51e1c147) ![A big square is completely shaded pink.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cd34526d153208c0fb2c5884b5ca657d51e1c147) ![A big square divided into 10 equal sized rectangles that are set in 10 rows of 1 rectangle each. 7 bottom rows with rectangles are shaded pink.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/32440b303ba4eb399711965a6da3f84011c9f9c6)
- | - | -
$\blue{\dfrac{217}{100}}:$ |![A big square divided into 100 equal sized squares that are set in 10 rows of 10 squares each. All 10 rows with 10 squares are shaded blue.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c375b2cc424c2b12a2b2a4da4517d72f99fa96bc) ![A big square divided into 100 equal sized squares that are set in 10 rows of 10 squares each. All 10 rows with 10 squares are shaded blue.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c375b2cc424c2b12a2b2a4da4517d72f99fa96bc) ![A big square divided into 100 equal sized squares that are set in 10 rows of 10 squares each. In the bottom row, all 10 squares are shaded blue. In the second row from the bottom, the left 7 squares are shaded blue.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2647a9558f6e1bcadaa46b42e64dd4b1d04c9b16)

Let's rewrite $\blue{\dfrac{217}{100}}$ as a decimal:

$\blue{\dfrac{217}{100}} = \blue{2 \text{ ones and } 17 \text{ hundredths}}= \blue{2.17}$
$\pink{2.7}$ covers **more** area than $\blue{2.17}$, so $\pink{2.7}$ is **greater** than $\blue{2.17}$.

Let's rewrite $\blue{5\text{ hundredths}}$ as a decimal number:

$\blue{5 \text{ hundredths}}= \blue{0.05}$

Now we can compare $\pink{0.5}$ and $\blue{0.05}$ with visuals.  
*(Each big square represents one whole.)*

$\pink{0.5}:$|![A big square divided into 10 equal sized rectangles that are set in 10 rows of 1 rectangle each. 5 bottom rows with rectangles are shaded pink.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/dc6ff0d28600d43c252eb29bfaccc1fcdcf9c8ec)
- | - | -
$\blue{0.05}:$ | ![A big square divided into 100 equal sized squares that are set in 10 rows of 10 squares each. In the bottom row, the left 5 squares are shaded blue.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c6118dce164337eec10870e35aad06d8e18bdc62)
$\pink{0.5}$ covers **more** area than $\blue{0.05}$, so $\pink{0.5}$ is **greater** than $\blue{0.05}$.
$\pink{0.5}> \blue{5 \text{ hundredths}}$

Let's rewrite $\blue{\dfrac{16}{10}}$ as a decimal number:

$\blue{\dfrac{16}{10}} = \blue{16 \text{ tenths}}= \blue{1.6}$

Now we can compare $\blue{1.6}$ and $\pink{0.61}$ with visuals.  
*(Each big square represents one whole.)*

$\blue{1.6}:$| ![A big square is completely shaded blue.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2608fd9f2206158155e1878d7011dff5ce40b91a) ![A big square divided into 10 equal sized rectangles that are set in 10 rows of 1 rectangle each. 6 bottom rows with rectangles are shaded blue.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/bbc388d7b97d061d3c0ce6f11dcfff61e6bb428b)
- | - | -
$\pink{0.61}:$ | ![A big square divided into 100 equal sized squares that are set in 10 rows of 10 squares each. In the bottom 6 rows, all 10 squares are shaded pink. In the seventh row from the bottom, 1 leftmost square is shaded pink.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ee9611969b16f6e71adc2e46e8f36f08e754d295)
$\blue{\dfrac{16}{10}}$ covers **more** area than $\pink{0.61}$, so $\blue{\dfrac{16}{10}}$ is **greater** than $\pink{0.61}$.
